chunks.title: video
chunks.text: next thing that we are going to learn about is operator precedence

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: operator precedence is basically priority order of our operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: whenever we have multiple operators in a single statement or expression

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for example if we have 3 multiplied by 5 plus 2

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then its answer can be either 17 or 5 plus 2 which is 7 into 3 which is 21

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this expression should have 17 or 21 answers which is our operator precedence

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: there are many other operators in operator precedence

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but we can keep in mind the precedence for the most commonly used operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: the highest precedence is of parenthesis

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for example if there is a parenthesis here 3 multiplied by 5 or 5 plus 2

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then the expression in parenthesis is solved

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this is the highest precedence

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: second number is of power

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: third number is of multiply, divide and modulo

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: fourth number is of plus and minus, subtraction and addition

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: after that we have relational operators and then our logical operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: logical operators come in last and in this also NOT comes first then AND then OR

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so in this expression we know that the higher precedence of multiply is as compared to plus

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so first of all we will multiply both which will be 15

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and in 15 we will add 2 so the answer is going to be 17

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so in this way operator precedence tells us that should we have priority order

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and this precedence is very similar to baud mass which we studied in math

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: the rules of baud mass were like bracket, open, divide, multiply, addition, subtraction

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this is the general rule which we follow

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: whenever two operators have same precedence

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: whenever two operators have same precedence

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so we solve our expression in left to right

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: left to right means if I have an expression 10 divided by 2 multiplied by 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so in this divide and multiply have same precedence

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so we will go left to right which means first we will divide 10 by 2 which will be 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and it will be multiplied by 5 so our answer will be 25

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so in this way we resolve our expressions with the help of operator precedence

text: next thing that we are going to learn about is operator precedence operator precedence is basically priority order of our operators whenever we have multiple operators in a single statement or expression for example if we have 3 multiplied by 5 plus 2 then its answer can be either 17 or 5 plus 2 which is 7 into 3 which is 21 so this expression should have 17 or 21 answers which is our operator precedence there are many other operators in operator precedence but we can keep in mind the precedence for the most commonly used operators the highest precedence is of parenthesis for example if there is a parenthesis here 3 multiplied by 5 or 5 plus 2 then the expression in parenthesis is solved so this is the highest precedence second number is of power third number is of multiply, divide and modulo fourth number is of plus and minus, subtraction and addition after that we have relational operators and then our logical operators logical operators come in last and in this also NOT comes first then AND then OR so in this expression we know that the higher precedence of multiply is as compared to plus so first of all we will multiply both which will be 15 and in 15 we will add 2 so the answer is going to be 17 so in this way operator precedence tells us that should we have priority order and this precedence is very similar to baud mass which we studied in math the rules of baud mass were like bracket, open, divide, multiply, addition, subtraction so this is the general rule which we follow whenever two operators have same precedence whenever two operators have same precedence so we solve our expression in left to right left to right means if I have an expression 10 divided by 2 multiplied by 5 so in this divide and multiply have same precedence so we will go left to right which means first we will divide 10 by 2 which will be 5 and it will be multiplied by 5 so our answer will be 25 so in this way we resolve our expressions with the help of operator precedence
